notes: Linear qualification. Builds on GCSE and A-Level Mathematics. All students study compulsory core pure content and choose TWO of three optional applications (Mechanics, Statistics, Discrete Mathematics). A calculator with iterative function, matrix operations up to 3×3, and statistical distribution capabilities is required.
overarching_themes: OT1: Mathematical argument, language and proof

papers.name: Paper 1
papers.description: Assesses any compulsory pure content (topics A-J). Mix of question styles from short single-mark to multi-step problems.

papers.name: Paper 2
papers.description: Assesses any compulsory pure content (topics A-J). Mix of question styles from short single-mark to multi-step problems.

papers.name: Paper 3
papers.description: Assesses the two optional applications chosen from Mechanics, Statistics, and Discrete Mathematics.

options.description: Students must study TWO of the following three options:

subtopics.name: Construct proofs using mathematical induction; contexts include sums of series, divisibility, and powers of matrices
Solve quadratic equations with real coefficients; solve cubic or quartic equations (given sufficient information)
Add, subtract, multiply and divide complex numbers in form x + iy; real part and imaginary part
Complex conjugate; non-real roots of polynomial equations with real coefficients occur in conjugate pairs
Argand diagrams - interpret and construct
Convert between Cartesian form and modulus-argument form
Multiply and divide complex numbers in modulus-argument form
Loci in the Argand diagram - z-a = r, arg(z-a) = θ
De Moivre's theorem - find multiple angle formulae and sums of series
Exponential form: e^(iθ) = cos θ + i sin θ; form z = re^(iθ)
Find n distinct n-th roots of re^(iθ) for r≠0; roots form vertices of regular n-gon
Use complex roots of unity to solve geometric problems
Add, subtract, multiply conformable matrices; multiply by a scalar
Zero and identity matrices
Linear transformations in 2D; successive transformations; single 3D transformations (reflection in x=0, y=0, z=0 or rotation about coordinate axes)
Invariant points and lines for a linear transformation
Determinants of 2×2 and 3×3 matrices; scale factors, effect on orientation
Singular and non-singular matrices; inverse of 2×2 and 3×3 matrices
Solve three linear simultaneous equations in three variables using inverse matrix
Geometrical interpretation of solution and failure of solution of three simultaneous equations
Factorisation of determinants using row and column operations
Eigenvalues and eigenvectors of 2×2 and 3×3 matrices; characteristic equation; geometrical significance
Diagonalisation of matrices: M = UDU⁻¹, Mⁿ = UDⁿU⁻¹ when eigenvalues are real
Relationship between roots and coefficients of polynomial equations up to quartic
Form polynomial equation whose roots are linear transformation of roots of given polynomial (at least cubic)
Formulae for sums of integers, squares and cubes; sum other series
Method of differences for summation of series including partial fractions
Maclaurin series of a function including general term
Maclaurin series for eˣ, ln(1+x), sin x, cos x, (1+x)ⁿ; range of validity
Evaluation of limits using Maclaurin series or L'Hôpital's rule
Inequalities involving polynomial equations (cubic and quartic)
Solving inequalities such as (ax+b)/(cx+d) < ex+f algebraically
Modulus of functions and associated inequalities
Graphs of y=|f(x)|, y=1/f(x) for given y=f(x)
Graphs of rational functions (ax+b)/(cx+d); asymptotes, intersections
Graphs of rational functions (ax²+bx+c)/(dx²+ex+f); oblique asymptotes
Quadratic theory to find possible values and stationary points of rational functions
Sketch curves: y²=4ax, x²/a²+y²/b²=1, x²/a²-y²/b²=1, xy=c²
Single and composite transformations including translations, stretches, reflections, rotations and enlargements
Evaluate improper integrals (undefined integrand or infinite range)
Volumes of revolution
Mean value of a function
Integration using partial fractions (including quadratic factors ax²+c)
Differentiate inverse trigonometric functions
Integrate functions of form (a²-x²)^(-½) and (a²+x²)^(-1); trigonometric substitutions
Arc length and area of surface of revolution (Cartesian or parametric)
Reduction formulae for integration
Limits: lim(x→∞) xᵏe⁻ˣ and lim(x→0) xᵏln x for k>0; application to improper integrals
Vector and Cartesian forms of equation of a straight line in 3D
Vector and Cartesian forms of equation of a plane
Scalar product; angle between two lines, equation of plane, angle between planes, angle between line and plane
Check whether vectors are perpendicular using scalar product
Vector product; equation (r-a)×b=0; area of triangle
Intersection of two lines; intersection of line and plane; perpendicular distance between lines, point to line, point to plane
Polar coordinates; convert between polar and Cartesian coordinates
Sketch curves with r given as function of θ including trigonometric functions
Find area enclosed by a polar curve
Definitions of sinh x, cosh x, tanh x, sech x, cosech x, coth x including domains and ranges; sketch graphs
Differentiate and integrate hyperbolic functions
Definitions of inverse hyperbolic functions; domains and ranges
Logarithmic forms of inverse hyperbolic functions
Integrate functions of form (x²+a²)^(-½) and (x²-a²)^(-½); substitutions
Hyperbolic identities: tanh x = sinh x/cosh x, cosh²x - sinh²x = 1, sech²x = 1 - tanh²x, etc.
Construct proofs involving hyperbolic functions and identities
Integrating factor for dy/dx + P(x)y = Q(x)
General and particular solutions of differential equations
Differential equations in modelling in kinematics and other contexts
Solve y'' + ay' + by = 0 using auxiliary equation
Solve y'' + ay' + by = f(x) by complementary function and particular integral (polynomial, exponential, trig)
Discriminant of auxiliary equation: positive, zero, negative cases
Simple harmonic motion ẍ = -ω²x
Damped oscillations; light, critical and heavy damping
Coupled first order simultaneous equations (e.g. predator-prey models)
Hooke's Law with T = kx to formulate SHM differential equation
Models for damped motion with damping proportional to velocity
Mid-ordinate rule and Simpson's rule for integration
Euler's step by step method for solving first order differential equations
Improved Euler method: yᵣ₊₁ = yᵣ₋₁ + 2hf(xᵣ,yᵣ), xᵣ₊₁ = xᵣ + h
Dimensions of quantities; dimensional consistency of equations
Predict formulae using dimensional analysis
Work done by force; principle of conservation of mechanical energy
Power; relationship between power, force and velocity
Work-energy principle including variable forces
Linear momentum; impulse = change in momentum
Conservation of linear momentum; direct collisions
Newton's experimental law (coefficient of restitution)
Oblique collisions
Centre of mass of a system of particles; uniform rods, laminas
Centre of mass of simple composite bodies
Equilibrium of rigid bodies; suspended bodies, toppling
Angular speed; motion in horizontal circle
Motion in vertical circle
Banked curves
Motion of a projectile under gravity (further work)
Motion in a resisting medium
Probability function, cumulative distribution function
Expectation and variance of discrete random variable
Discrete uniform distribution
Geometric distribution
Conditions, notation, probabilities, mean and variance
Sum of independent Poisson distributions
Poisson approximation to Binomial distribution

topics.name: Type I and Type II Errors

subtopics.name: Type I and Type II errors in hypothesis testing
Significance level and power of a test
Sample size calculations
Continuous random variables - probability density function, cumulative distribution function
Expectation and variance of continuous random variables
Continuous uniform distribution
Exponential distribution
Goodness of fit tests
Contingency tables
Degrees of freedom; Yates' correction
Graph terminology - vertices, edges, trails, paths, cycles, subgraphs, trees
Eulerian trails and tours; Hamiltonian cycles
Planarity; Euler's formula v - e + f = 2
Graph colouring; chromatic number
Network terminology - weighted graph, digraph
Minimum spanning tree: Kruskal's and Prim's algorithms
Shortest path: Dijkstra's algorithm
Chinese postman problem
Travelling salesman problem - nearest neighbour, lower bounds, minimum tour
Flows in networks - capacities, sources, sinks
Cuts and their capacities
Maximum flow-minimum cut theorem
Multiple sources and sinks
Formulate LP problems
Graphical solution of two-variable problems
Simplex algorithm
Two-stage simplex and big-M method
Activity networks, precedences, dummy activities
Earliest and latest start/finish times
Critical path, total float, independent float
Gantt charts, scheduling
Two-person zero-sum games, pay-off matrices
Play-safe strategies, saddle points
Optimal mixed strategies
Binary operations, commutativity, associativity
Identity and inverse elements
Group theory - definition of a group, subgroups, Lagrange's theorem
First and second order linear recurrence relations
Solution of homogeneous and particular solutions
Modelling with recurrence relations
